channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Wildfires latest: Firefighters prepare for dry conditions and strong winds
item.description: Firefighters around Los Angeles were preparing on Tuesday to attack flare-ups or new blazes. The National Weather Service issued a rare warning that dry winds combined with severely dry conditions created a “ particularly dangerous situation," indicating that any new fire could explode in size.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Contaminated drinking water is a growing concern for cities facing wildfires
item.description: As fires continue to burn across Los Angeles, several utilities have declared their drinking water unsafe until extensive testing can prove otherwise. A warmer, drier climate means wildfires are getting worse, and encroaching on cities — with devastating impact.
item.dc:creator: BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Kate returns to cancer center where she was treated to offer gratitude to medical teams
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The Princess of Wales visited the hospital where she was treated for cancer on Tuesday, offering gratitude to the medical teams who helped her and give support to patients now undergoing treatment.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: What to know about the devastation from the Los Angeles-area fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fires burning homes and businesses in Los Angeles for a week have killed at least 24 people, displaced thousands of others and destroyed more than 12,000 buildings in what might be the most expensive conflagrations in the nation's history. The blazes started Jan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump claims California had the chance to 'maintain their forests' amid Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: “The problem is it's so dry, it was always so dry there, and it's just … it's just a mess. They could’ve, you know, maintained their forests, and — as I said they have to do,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: Firefighters deploy widely as strong winds threaten more Los Angeles neighborhoods
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Weary and anxious from a week of massive infernos burning around Los Angeles and now facing dire new warnings, residents gathered up their pets and family photos and prepared to flee at a moment's notice as fire-fueling winds blasted Tuesday across Southern California.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, JULIE WATSON and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Hamas has accepted a draft agreement for a Gaza ceasefire and the release of hostages, officials say
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Hamas has accepted a draft agreement for a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip and the release of dozens of hostages, two officials involved in the talks said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Poll reveals how much voters blame climate change, Newsom for California fire crisis
item.description: In the wake of several deadly California wildfires a new poll shows how Americans are reacting to the disaster, which left at least two dozen people dead and reduced entire neighborhoods to charred rubble.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Small airborne embers play a big role in the spread of wildfires
item.description: While authorities still don’t know what sparked the deadly fires in the Los Angeles area, they do know one clear way the flames have spread: embers. At least 24 people have died in the fires that have destroyed more than 12,000 structures since starting last Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 600 wooden crosses honor migrants who died crossing the border in 2024
item.description: A field of 600 wooden crosses -- representing migrants who died while trying to cross the border in 2024 -- were taken up in a border ceremony held this weekend in Eagle Pass, Texas.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: How Trump's Greenland demands could impact Ozempic costs
item.description: President-elect Trump's threat to tariff Denmark if it resists his acquisition plans for the island territory of Greenland could disrupt one export that is wildly popular in America: Ozempic.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, NewsNation

item.title: Firefighters deploy as strong winds threaten more LA neighborhoods
item.description: A beefed-up firefighting force was staged to attack flareups or new blazes, a week after the first still-burning fires began and destroyed thousands of homes and killed at least 24 people.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Smith defends Trump prosecution as final Jan. 6 report released to the public
item.description: Special counsel Jack Smith defended his investigations into President-elect Trump in a final report released publicly early Tuesday morning, saying his determinations were free from political interference and that, if permitted to proceed, his cases would have likely secured a conviction.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: Sheriff calls on wildfire evacuees to be patient amid 'grim' search for bodies
item.description: Los Angeles County Sheriff Robert Luna asked community members to be patient with local officials, as residents eagerly await permission to return to their homes in southern California, where tens of thousands of acres have been decimated by wildfires in the past week.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: Los Angeles price gouging is 'inhumane': Real estate agent
item.description: Nikko Santo Pietro, Los Angeles real estate agent and son of 'Wheel of Fortune’s' Vanna White, said the supply of housing is “so limited.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, KRON4

item.title: Victims react to new intelligence community report that Havana Syndrome could be caused by a foreign actor
item.description: Some intelligence agencies are now suggesting a foreign adversary may be behind the mysterious "Havana Syndrome" injuries reportedly sustained by U.S. officials overseas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Colorado apartment complex where armed gang members were seen on video will be closed
item.description: AURORA, Colo. (AP) — A Colorado apartment complex where armed members of a Venezuelan gang were caught on video entering a unit last summer is expected to close in about a month under an emergency court order, city officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suspected looter dressed as fireman detained in Los Angeles
item.description: Authorities say nearly a dozen people have been arrested with "burglary tools" since the fires broke out.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, NewsNation

item.title: Hanging out at Starbucks? It will cost you as company reverses its open-door policy
item.description: (AP) — If you want to hang out or use the restroom at Starbucks, you’re going to have to buy something. Starbucks on Monday said it was reversing a policy that invited everyone into its stores.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mother of former child star killed in Palisades Fire speaks on devastating loss
item.description: Shelley Sykes fought back tears recalling the final moments with 32-year-old Rory, who was born blind and lived with cerebral palsy.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, NewsNation

item.title: Los Angeles wildfires: California police arrest multiple drone pilots as firefighters battle infernos
item.description: Los Angeles police arrested three people following drone incidents in wildfire areas as first responders battle infernos raging in Southern California.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Carrie Underwood to perform at Trump inauguration
item.description: Country music star Carrie Underwood is set to take the stage on Inauguration Day, a sign that President-elect Trump's second inauguration may be drawing more high-profile talent than his first go round.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Teacher arrested after threat that shut down NJ school district, police say
item.description: A New Jersey teacher has been arrested after a potential threat to staff and students shut down an entire school district on Monday, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Visual impairment doesn't stop man from running sports podcast, bowling league
item.description: "One day a few of us got together and said, 'Let's start a podcast. Let's start a sports show,'" Bob Branco recalled. "We used Skype, but now we use Zoom.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bawden, NewsNation

item.title: UFC champion booted from Frontier flight after being told he couldn't sit in exit row
item.description: Former UFC lightweight champion Khabib Nurmagomedov was removed from a Frontier Airways flight on Saturday after refusing to state whether he would be able to assist other travelers in the event of an emergency, a spokesperson for Frontier told Nexstar on
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Photos: Handful of L.A. homes remain untouched amid charred neighborhoods
item.description: The Walsh House, featured in the “Beverly Hills 90210" television series, survived. So did its pristine lawn. Next door, the remains of a neighbor's home was blackened with soot.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Firm has no plans to salvage more Titanic artifacts, snuffing out legal fight
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — The U.S. government has scrapped its litigation against the company that owns the salvage rights to the Titanic, noting that the firm no longer has expedition plans to the shipwreck that could break federal law.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Texas man accused of sending Caitlin Clark violent messages on X: court docs
item.description: A Texas man is accused of sending sexually violent messages from his X account to Indiana Fever star Caitlin Clark and then driving to Indianapolis, court documents reveal.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Taquitos sold at Aldi recalled after customer complains of 'dental injury': USDA
item.description: The recall specifically concerns 20-ounce packages of Casa Mamita Chicken & Cheese Taquitos produced by Bestway Sandwiches, Inc.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's inauguration weekend features fireworks, MAGA rally, inaugural balls
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump's inauguration weekend will start with a fireworks display outside his northern Virginia golf club and end with a trio of inaugural balls where he will speak, the committee planning the events announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Newsom concerned Trump will try to withhold California fire aid
item.description: “He’s done it in Utah. He’s done it in Michigan, did it in Puerto Rico. He did it to California back before I was even governor, in 2018, until he found out folks in Orange County voted for him and he decided to give them money.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KRON4

item.title: PHOTOS: West Virginia father-son duo build ice dragon igloo
item.description: While many of us were staying cozy indoors, a father-son duo was hard at work making the best of this week's wintery weather by building a one-of-a-kind igloo.
item.dc:creator: Sam Gorski, ABC 4

item.title: New rule could slash nicotine levels in tobacco products
item.description: The Biden administration is poised to try to lower the amount of nicotine in tobacco products, an eleventh-hour effort that’s been years in the making.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Illegal immigrant with $1M worth of drugs, guns given free housing courtesy of blue state taxpayers: officials
item.description: An illegal immigrant from the Dominican Republic was allegedly caught with $1 million worth of fentanyl and an AR-15 rifle in Massachusetts' emergency housing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hurricane Katrina victim forced to evacuate again, this time for Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: The California wildfires are hitting too close to home for a woman who used to live in New Orleans. She was forced to evacuate during Hurricane Katrina, and now she's evacuating once again.
item.dc:creator: Kenny Lopez, KTLA 5

item.title: Lake Superior State's 2025 Banished Words List
item.description: Lake Superior State University's 2025 list of banned words includes "cringe", "game changer", "era", "dropped", "IYKYK", "sorry not sorry", "skibidi", "100%", "utilize", and "period", reflecting the dynamic nature of communication and the need to be mindful of the language used.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Trump’s bid to delay sentencing in his New York hush money case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A sharply divided Supreme Court on Thursday rejected President-elect Donald Trump’s final bid to put his New York hush-money case on hold, clearing the way for him to be sentenced for felony crimes days before he returns to the presidency. The court’s 5-4 order allows Judge Juan M.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, MICHAEL HILL and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4
